title: Ev Charger Buildout Acceleration Bullish Play
thesis: Public EV charger network expansion correlates with infrastructure investment cycles and consumer EV adoption confidence. Rapid week-over-week growth in charger availability signals macro tailwind for energy transition. Charger installation is industrial capex; growth signals infrastructure spending momentum benefiting industrial equipment and construction.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region through openchargemap adapter
signal_logic: If weekly charger count increase exceeds prior 8-week rolling average by 15% or more
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly charger count increase exceeds prior 8-week rolling average by 15% or more Exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly growth falls below 5% for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region through openchargemap adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger deployment fluctuates seasonally and by funding cycles; threshold of 15% growth above 8-week MA fires 2–3 times per quarter.

schema.idea: EV Charger Buildout Acceleration Bullish Play

data.source: OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region through openchargemap adapter

schema.behavior: Public EV charger network expansion correlates with infrastructure investment cycles and consumer EV adoption confidence. Rapid week-over-week growth in charger availability signals macro tailwind for energy transition.

market_impact.rationale: Charger installation is industrial capex; growth signals infrastructure spending momentum benefiting industrial equipment and construction.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly charger count increase exceeds prior 8-week rolling average by 15% or more
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly growth falls below 5% for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger deployment fluctuates seasonally and by funding cycles; threshold of 15% growth above 8-week MA fires 2–3 times per quarter.

title: Natural Gas Price Spike Industrial Input Stress Signal
thesis: Natural gas price spikes compress margins for chemical, fertilizer, and power generation firms. Sharp intraweek rallies often reverse within 1–2 weeks as supply/demand rebalances. Industrial production and utilities are major gas consumers; price shocks raise input costs and trigger margin compression.
data_sources: FRED series ID DHHNGSP (Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price) through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If natural gas price rises more than 8% in a single week and closes above 5-week high
entry_exit: Entry: If natural gas price rises more than 8% in a single week and closes above 5-week high Exit: After 7 trading days or if price falls below the 5-week moving average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ID DHHNGSP (Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price) through fred_series adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Natural gas is volatile; 8% weekly moves occur 3–4 times per quarter, especially in winter and summer demand spikes.

schema.idea: Natural Gas Price Spike Industrial Input Stress Signal

data.source: FRED series ID DHHNGSP (Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price) through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: Natural gas price spikes compress margins for chemical, fertilizer, and power generation firms. Sharp intraweek rallies often reverse within 1–2 weeks as supply/demand rebalances.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial production and utilities are major gas consumers; price shocks raise input costs and trigger margin compression.

trade_logic.entry: If natural gas price rises more than 8% in a single week and closes above 5-week high
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or if price falls below the 5-week moving average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Natural gas is volatile; 8% weekly moves occur 3–4 times per quarter, especially in winter and summer demand spikes.

title: Unemployment Spike Google Trends Job Search Urgency
thesis: Sudden spikes in unemployment-related and urgent job search queries precede or coincide with labor market deterioration. High search volume indicates worker stress and hiring slowdown. Job insecurity depresses consumer discretionary spending; labor stress reduces purchasing power.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'job openings near me' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If combined search index for both terms rises above 70th percentile of 52-week range in a single week
entry_exit: Entry: If combined search index for both terms rises above 70th percentile of 52-week range in a single week Exit: After 14 calendar days or if index falls back below 50th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'job openings near me' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Job market anxiety spikes occur monthly around earnings announcements, layoff waves, and macro uncertainty; threshold is loose enough to fire 2–3 times per month.

schema.idea: Unemployment Spike Google Trends Job Search Urgency

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'unemployment benefits' and 'job openings near me' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Sudden spikes in unemployment-related and urgent job search queries precede or coincide with labor market deterioration. High search volume indicates worker stress and hiring slowdown.

market_impact.rationale: Job insecurity depresses consumer discretionary spending; labor stress reduces purchasing power.

trade_logic.entry: If combined search index for both terms rises above 70th percentile of 52-week range in a single week
trade_logic.exit: After 14 calendar days or if index falls back below 50th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Job market anxiety spikes occur monthly around earnings announcements, layoff waves, and macro uncertainty; threshold is loose enough to fire 2–3 times per month.

title: Freight Bill Of Lading Cost Inflation Pressure
thesis: When inventory growth outpaces sales growth, logistics firms hold excess cargo and rates compress. Sudden sales acceleration forces rapid restocking, spiking freight demand and rates. Freight logistics (rail, trucking, shipping) benefit from cargo volume spikes and higher utilization rates.
data_sources: FRED series ID TOTALSA (Total Business Inventories) and FRED EXCRESW (Advance Retail Sales) ratio divergence through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If weekly inventory-to-sales ratio drops below 2-month low and retail sales surprise positively by 2%+ week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly inventory-to-sales ratio drops below 2-month low and retail sales surprise positively by 2%+ week-over-week Exit: After 10 trading days or if inventory ratio rises back above 2-month moving average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ID TOTALSA (Total Business Inventories) and FRED EXCRESW (Advance Retail Sales) ratio divergence through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Inventory cycles reset quarterly; sales surprises and ratio inversions fire 2–4 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Freight Bill Of Lading Cost Inflation Pressure

data.source: FRED series ID TOTALSA (Total Business Inventories) and FRED EXCRESW (Advance Retail Sales) ratio divergence through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: When inventory growth outpaces sales growth, logistics firms hold excess cargo and rates compress. Sudden sales acceleration forces rapid restocking, spiking freight demand and rates.

market_impact.rationale: Freight logistics (rail, trucking, shipping) benefit from cargo volume spikes and higher utilization rates.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly inventory-to-sales ratio drops below 2-month low and retail sales surprise positively by 2%+ week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if inventory ratio rises back above 2-month moving average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Inventory cycles reset quarterly; sales surprises and ratio inversions fire 2–4 times per quarter.

title: Travel Mobility Pent-up Demand Google Trends Surge
thesis: Sharp spikes in travel booking searches indicate sudden pent-up demand release, often following lockdowns, price drops, or seasonal transitions. Correlates with airline and hospitality stock strength. Travel and leisure are discretionary; search spikes signal consumer confidence and spending willingness.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'vacation packages', 'flight deals', and 'hotel bookings' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If combined travel search index exceeds 65th percentile of 13-week rolling window
entry_exit: Entry: If combined travel search index exceeds 65th percentile of 13-week rolling window Exit: After 12 trading days or if index falls to below 40th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'vacation packages', 'flight deals', and 'hotel bookings' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Travel searches spike seasonally (holidays, spring break, summer) and after price drops; threshold fires 2–3 times per month.

schema.idea: Travel Mobility Pent-Up Demand Google Trends Surge

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'vacation packages', 'flight deals', and 'hotel bookings' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Sharp spikes in travel booking searches indicate sudden pent-up demand release, often following lockdowns, price drops, or seasonal transitions. Correlates with airline and hospitality stock strength.

market_impact.rationale: Travel and leisure are discretionary; search spikes signal consumer confidence and spending willingness.

trade_logic.entry: If combined travel search index exceeds 65th percentile of 13-week rolling window
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or if index falls to below 40th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Travel searches spike seasonally (holidays, spring break, summer) and after price drops; threshold fires 2–3 times per month.

title: Copper Price Momentum Mean Reversion Industrial Cycle
thesis: Copper is a leading cyclical indicator. Sharp 3–5 day rallies above the 50-day moving average often reverse within a week as overextension unwinds, creating mean reversion trades. Copper strength signals manufacturing demand and infrastructure investment; materials and industrials benefit.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for CU (Copper Futures ETF) or commodity copper index through price_only adapter
signal_logic: If copper price rallies 4%+ in 3 trading days and closes above 50-day MA by 2%+
entry_exit: Entry: If copper price rallies 4%+ in 3 trading days and closes above 50-day MA by 2%+ Exit: After 6 trading days or if price falls back below 50-day MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for CU (Copper Futures ETF) or commodity copper index through price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Copper rallies 4%+ in 3 days occur 1–2 times per month on cyclical swings and Fed commentary.

schema.idea: Copper Price Momentum Mean Reversion Industrial Cycle

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for CU (Copper Futures ETF) or commodity copper index through price_only adapter

schema.behavior: Copper is a leading cyclical indicator. Sharp 3–5 day rallies above the 50-day moving average often reverse within a week as overextension unwinds, creating mean reversion trades.

market_impact.rationale: Copper strength signals manufacturing demand and infrastructure investment; materials and industrials benefit.

trade_logic.entry: If copper price rallies 4%+ in 3 trading days and closes above 50-day MA by 2%+
trade_logic.exit: After 6 trading days or if price falls back below 50-day MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Copper rallies 4%+ in 3 days occur 1–2 times per month on cyclical swings and Fed commentary.

title: Seismic Activity Uptick Insurance Realty Volatility Spike
thesis: Clusters of seismic events in major metro regions (CA, WA, OK) trigger insurance claim anticipation and property revaluation concerns. Real estate and insurance stocks respond to perceived risk. Earthquake clusters depress property values and increase insurance costs; REIT valuations compress.
data_sources: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program API daily earthquake counts (magnitude 3.0+) by region through earthquake_activity adapter
signal_logic: If weekly count of magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes in CA+WA+OK exceeds 52-week 75th percentile (typical: 3+ events)
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly count of magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes in CA+WA+OK exceeds 52-week 75th percentile (typical: 3+ events) Exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly earthquake count returns to median for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS Earthquake Hazards Program API daily earthquake counts (magnitude 3.0+) by region through earthquake_activity adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic clusters occur unpredictably but reliably; threshold fires 1–2 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Seismic Activity Uptick Insurance & Realty Volatility Spike

data.source: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program API daily earthquake counts (magnitude 3.0+) by region through earthquake_activity adapter

schema.behavior: Clusters of seismic events in major metro regions (CA, WA, OK) trigger insurance claim anticipation and property revaluation concerns. Real estate and insurance stocks respond to perceived risk.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake clusters depress property values and increase insurance costs; REIT valuations compress.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly count of magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes in CA+WA+OK exceeds 52-week 75th percentile (typical: 3+ events)
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days or if weekly earthquake count returns to median for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic clusters occur unpredictably but reliably; threshold fires 1–2 times per quarter.

title: Medical Care Cost Search Spike Consumer Health Stress
thesis: Spikes in healthcare cost and debt-related searches indicate consumer financial stress from medical expenses. Precedes credit card delinquencies and reduced discretionary spending. Medical cost stress reduces disposable income and consumer confidence, depressing discretionary sector.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'medical debt', 'prescription drug cost', 'health insurance denied claim' through google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If combined healthcare cost search index rises above 60th percentile of 26-week rolling window
entry_exit: Entry: If combined healthcare cost search index rises above 60th percentile of 26-week rolling window Exit: After 14 calendar days or if index falls below 40th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'medical debt', 'prescription drug cost', 'health insurance denied claim' through google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost searches spike during open enrollment, post-deductible resets (Jan), and high-inflation periods; fires 2–3 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Medical Care Cost Search Spike Consumer Health Stress

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'medical debt', 'prescription drug cost', 'health insurance denied claim' through google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Spikes in healthcare cost and debt-related searches indicate consumer financial stress from medical expenses. Precedes credit card delinquencies and reduced discretionary spending.

market_impact.rationale: Medical cost stress reduces disposable income and consumer confidence, depressing discretionary sector.

trade_logic.entry: If combined healthcare cost search index rises above 60th percentile of 26-week rolling window
trade_logic.exit: After 14 calendar days or if index falls below 40th percentile for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost searches spike during open enrollment, post-deductible resets (Jan), and high-inflation periods; fires 2–3 times per quarter.

title: Fed Liquidity Drain Rate Shock Dollar Strength Rally
thesis: Sharp increases in Fed reverse repo usage or Treasury bill drains signal sudden liquidity tightening. Dollar strengthens, emerging markets weaken, commodity prices compress. Dollar strength and liquidity drains compress commodity and emerging market revenues; energy exporters underperform.
data_sources: FRED series ID WIMFSL (Factors Affecting Reserve Balances – Liquidity Drain) through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If Fed liquidity drain (weekly average) increases 10%+ versus prior week and closes at 10-week high
entry_exit: Entry: If Fed liquidity drain (weekly average) increases 10%+ versus prior week and closes at 10-week high Exit: After 8 trading days or if drain rate falls 5% below the spike peak
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ID WIMFSL (Factors Affecting Reserve Balances – Liquidity Drain) through fred_series adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Liquidity drains occur with quarter-end, tax date, and seasonal treasury issuance cycles; 10% spikes fire 2–3 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Fed Liquidity Drain Rate Shock Dollar Strength Rally

data.source: FRED series ID WIMFSL (Factors Affecting Reserve Balances – Liquidity Drain) through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: Sharp increases in Fed reverse repo usage or Treasury bill drains signal sudden liquidity tightening. Dollar strengthens, emerging markets weaken, commodity prices compress.

market_impact.rationale: Dollar strength and liquidity drains compress commodity and emerging market revenues; energy exporters underperform.

trade_logic.entry: If Fed liquidity drain (weekly average) increases 10%+ versus prior week and closes at 10-week high
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or if drain rate falls 5% below the spike peak

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Liquidity drains occur with quarter-end, tax date, and seasonal treasury issuance cycles; 10% spikes fire 2–3 times per quarter.

title: Credit Card Application Volume Surge Consumer Confidence Inflection
thesis: Sudden spikes in credit card applications signal consumer confidence inflection. Rising applications precede consumer spending rallies and credit card company profitability expansion. Credit card volume and approval rates directly impact bank and fintech lending profitability.
data_sources: FRED series ID TERMCBCCALLNS (Credit Card Applications Approved) through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If weekly credit card applications approved exceed 8-week moving average by 12%+ and trend higher for 2 weeks
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly credit card applications approved exceed 8-week moving average by 12%+ and trend higher for 2 weeks Exit: After 15 trading days or if approvals fall back below 8-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series ID TERMCBCCALLNS (Credit Card Applications Approved) through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Card application cycles reset with holiday shopping, back-to-school, and quarterly rate change announcements; fires 2–4 times per quarter.

schema.idea: Credit Card Application Volume Surge Consumer Confidence Inflection

data.source: FRED series ID TERMCBCCALLNS (Credit Card Applications Approved) through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: Sudden spikes in credit card applications signal consumer confidence inflection. Rising applications precede consumer spending rallies and credit card company profitability expansion.

market_impact.rationale: Credit card volume and approval rates directly impact bank and fintech lending profitability.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly credit card applications approved exceed 8-week moving average by 12%+ and trend higher for 2 weeks
trade_logic.exit: After 15 trading days or if approvals fall back below 8-week MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Card application cycles reset with holiday shopping, back-to-school, and quarterly rate change announcements; fires 2–4 times per quarter.
